title: Webhook key compared with non-constant-time ==
description: In webhook.rs:53, the webhook authentication key is compared with `*expected_key == query.key`. Rust's `String` PartialEq short-circuits on the first differing byte, creating a timing side-channel. An attacker who can make many webhook requests can binary-search the correct key one byte at a time. This is a P0 because the webhook key is the sole access-control gate for creating and federating posts. Fix: use a constant-time comparison such as `subtle::ConstantTimeEq` on the byte slices, or `ring::constant_time::verify_slices_are_equal`. Add the `subtle` crate (already a transitive dep via rsa) and compare `expected_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(query.key.as_bytes()).into()`.
close_reason: P0 fixes: AtomicU64, UTF-8 path, OsRng, 410 cleanup, constant-time key compare, checked_mul, SSRF guard, sanitized URLs, persona exists check, UTF-8 safe truncate
